title: Molten Bitter French Bread Tartiflette
description: A crowd-pleasing French dish featuring molten bread and a tandoor, free from onions and garlic, this tartiflette is perfect for any occasion. It combines the bitterness of certain ingredients with the richness of molten cheese.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the French bread into 1-inch cubes and toast until lightly browned.
In a large skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat and saute the mushrooms until tender.
Add the diced bitter melon to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a separate pot, boil the diced potatoes until they are tender, then drain and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the toasted bread, sauteed mushrooms and bitter melon, and boiled potatoes.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and top with Reblochon cheese.
Dot the top of the cheese with the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
